target's body is protected — nearly all, partial- ly, or just a bit, Reduce the skill number as, the Combat Modifiers Chart directs. Ht Movement 
‘When a character attacks while standing still or strolling his skill number is not modified. ‘When he is walking or running, its reduced, as the chart indicates. When the target is mov- ing, the attackers skill is also reduced. eae) Hit Location When a characteris hit in combat you can, if you like, roll on the Hit Location Table (see Fold-Out 6) to see where his wound is located Use your judgment about this; for instance, i someone is stunned, who cares where the shot hit (if anywhere)? Similarly, if he's dead or vaporized, it's a mite moot, and even if he's incapacitated, he's not going anywhere, so ‘who cares whether it’s a hit in the head or the abdomen? Too, witha flamethrower for exam ple, precise hit location may be irrelevant — the damage may be equally distributed over lots of the body. ‘When a characteris wounded in a patcular location, not only does he suffer a-4 penalty ‘onal skill and attribute rolls, but he loses the use ofthat part of his body, “Lose the use of can mean a number of diferent things ‘Arm: Can't use that arm. Can't carry heavy things, Can't use weapons that require the use of both arms leg: Can't move faster than a walk (hop, hop). No bike riding, either. Chest: Oops. Better sit down. You're not yo- ing anywhere, although you can still fire ‘weapons and use skis, ‘Abdomen: Like chest, only more painful Head: Whoa, Bad news. What “ose the use cof” means is upto you. It ight mean he can't see anything — either because he's blinded Cr because blood is running into his ees.
